visual_attributes.morphology: elongated, thin blades of hardened snow or ice
distinctive_features: pointing towards the general direction of the sun
resemble a crowd of kneeling people
visual_attributes.texture: hard and dry at the peaks, wet in the troughs
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: ranging from a few centimeters to over 5 meters (16 ft)
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: nearly vertical wedges oriented east-west, parallel to the solar beam

domain_specific_visual.tilt_direction: tilted toward the north in the Southern Hemisphere

functional_attributes.primary_function: affect the energy balance of the snow surface

domain_specific_functional.sublimation_process: peaks remain dry and cold by sublimation

relationships.description: Penitentes are commonly found in the Dry Andes above 4,000 meters.
First described in scientific literature by Charles Darwin in 1839.
Penitentes have been suggested to exist on Pluto, composed of methane ice.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: From the Cascades at 50°N to the southern Andes at 40°S
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Resemble the tall, pointed habits and hoods worn by brothers of religious orders during Spanish Holy Week
